admin.title: Platform admin
admin.subtitle: Logged in as platform administrator ({{email}}). All state-changing actions are written to the audit log.
admin.grantReasonHint: Visible to the customer in their billing page. Keep it short and explanatory ("Beta tester appreciation", "Refund credit for outage").

bonus.title: {{plan}} courtesy upgrade is active 🎁
bonus.body: Your plan is set to {{plan}} as a complimentary upgrade until {{until}} ({{untilRel}}). All paid features are unlocked. Caps and retention follow the bonus tier — billing will revert to the regular cadence afterwards.

orgSwitcher.label: Organization
orgSwitcher.deleteWarning: This is permanent. All projects, issues, events, alert rules, and destinations under this organization will be deleted.
orgSwitcher.deleteBody: Type the organization name to confirm.

auth.loginHint: Sign in with your organization account

onboarding.title: Create a project
onboarding.errorOrgName: Organization name must be at least 2 characters
onboarding.errorProjectName: Project name must be at least 2 characters
onboarding.successDsnHint: Copy this DSN into your app's config — it's the only way the SDK can authenticate to ingest.

projects.title: Projects
projects.empty: No projects yet — create one to start receiving events.
projects.orgNotFound: Organization not found in your memberships.
projects.archiveBody: Archive "{{name}}"? It will disappear from the project list, but its issues and events stay accessible by direct ID for incident review.
projects.archiveHint: This action is reversible from the database, but no UI to unarchive yet.
projects.dsnHint: Copy this DSN into your app's config — it's the only way the SDK can authenticate to ingest.

connect.title: Connect
connect.subtitle: Paste-ready config for every supported client. Copy the DSN into SDKs, the PAT into MCP servers and CLI — same project, two credentials, ten snippets.
connect.notFound: Project not found in this organization.

dsn.title: DSN
dsn.hint: Public credential safe to ship in client code. Authenticates ingest for THIS project only — cannot read issues, manage projects, or call admin APIs.
dsn.empty: This project has no active DSN yet — generate one to start receiving events.

pat.title: Personal access token
pat.hint: Private credential carrying your full membership scope. Required for MCP servers, the CLI, and any script that reads or writes through the API. Mint a fresh one below — the plaintext is shown exactly once.
pat.generate: Generate a PAT for this flow
pat.oneTimeWarning: Copy this now — it will never be shown again. Anyone with this token can act as you within the scopes you selected.

snippets.title: Paste-ready snippets
snippets.hint: Pick your stack and click Copy. Snippets fill in your DSN / PAT automatically; missing credentials show a placeholder so you can still copy the structure.

projectKeys.title: DSN keys
projectKeys.subtitle: Each key authenticates SDK traffic to ingest. Generate a new one to rotate; revoke any key you don't recognize.
projectKeys.useHint: Use DSN keys in your client SDK config (browser, mobile, server SDKs) to send events to ingest. They have one job — accepting event traffic for THIS project. They cannot read issues, manage projects, or call admin APIs. For those, generate a Personal Access Token from your account menu.
projectKeys.empty: No active keys yet — generate one to start receiving events.
projectKeys.revokeBody: After revoking, any SDK still configured with this DSN will start getting 401s. The action cannot be undone — generate a fresh key for the impacted service first if you need uninterrupted ingest.
projectKeys.revealedHint: This is the only time the full DSN will be shown. Copy it now — after closing this dialog the dashboard can only display its metadata, and no endpoint can ever return the secret again.

issues.title: Issues
issues.empty: No issues — your code is healthy.
issues.invalidProjectId: Invalid project — pick a project from the sidebar.

alertDestinations.title: Alert destinations
alertDestinations.empty: No destinations yet — add one so alert rules have somewhere to fire.
alertDestinations.kindLocked: Kind cannot be changed after create — make a new destination instead.
alertDestinations.name: Name
alertDestinations.configCreateHint: Per-kind shape — e.g. Telegram needs chatId, Slack needs webhookUrl. Stored encrypted.
alertDestinations.configEditHint: Re-enter the full config to save. The previous config is encrypted server-side and not shown back.
alertDestinations.errorConfigShape: Config must be a JSON object

alertRules.title: Alert rules
alertRules.empty: No alert rules yet — create one to start firing notifications on incoming issues.
alertRules.name: Name
alertRules.conditionsHint: DSL: level.in, firstSeenWindow (ISO-8601), occurrenceThreshold, tag.{key,in}. All AND-ed.
alertRules.throttleHint: Skip firing again within this window. 0 disables throttling.
alertRules.needDestinations: Add a destination first — rules need somewhere to fan out to.
alertRules.errorConditionsShape: Conditions must be a JSON object

billing.title: Billing
billing.capExceeded: Monthly event cap exceeded — new events are dropped until next renewal.
billing.paymentFailedBody: Update your payment method within {{days}} day(s) — otherwise your plan will be downgraded to Free.
billing.cryptoNote: USDT, USDC, BTC, ETH and 200+ tokens accepted. Hosted by NOWPayments.
billing.renewSoon: Plan expires in {{days}} day(s) — renew below to keep Pro.
billing.renewExpired: Plan expired — purchase a new term to restore Pro features.
billing.tierSdksLine: All SDKs (browser, React, Node, Java, Python, …)
billing.currentBalance: You're already on this plan — extending will add to your renewal date.
billing.downgradeWarning: This is a lower tier than your current plan.

releases.title: Releases
releases.intro: Tag deploy versions to attribute issues to a specific build. Upload source maps against a release for symbolicated stack traces.
releases.versionHint: Free-form string. Common patterns: semver (1.2.3), git SHA (a1b2c3d), or calver (2026.05.08).
releases.deleteBody: Source-map artifacts uploaded against this release will be removed too. R2 blobs stay until the next maintenance sweep.
releases.empty: No releases yet — create one to start attributing issues to deploy versions.

members.title: Members
members.intro: Owners can invite people to this organization and manage their roles. Invitees get an email with a link to sign in; if they don't have an account yet, the access binds automatically when they create one with this email.
members.inviteHint: They'll get an email pointing to the dashboard. Their access takes effect on first sign-in.
members.removeBody: They'll lose access to every project in this organization. PATs they own continue to work until revoked separately.
members.leaveBody: You'll lose access to every project here. You can be re-invited later by another owner.
members.empty: Just you so far. Invite teammates to share visibility into projects.

tokens.title: Personal access tokens
tokens.intro: Create a token to authenticate the CLI or scripts as you. Tokens carry your full membership scope, so treat them like passwords.
tokens.useHint: Use PATs for the MCP server, the CLI, and any server-side script that calls the Arguslog API on your behalf. Unlike a DSN key (which can only send events to a single project), a PAT can read issues, manage projects, and call admin endpoints across every org you belong to — subject to the scopes you select below.
tokens.issuedHint: Copy this now — it will not be shown again.
tokens.empty: No tokens yet — create one above.
tokens.revokeBody: This is irreversible. Any client using this token will start receiving 401 immediately.
tokens.scopesHint: Restrict what this token can do. Leaving every scope checked is the same as a fully-privileged token.
